validation_evidence.command: node --input-type=module -e "const m = await import('./test/helpers/control-plane-fixture.js'); const f = m.createControlPlaneFixture(); try { if (!f.env.LAUNCHDECK_HOME || f.env.LAUNCHDECK_HOME !== f.homeDir) throw new Error('isolated home env missing'); if (!m.fixtureServerScriptContent().includes('createServer')) throw new Error('server script content missing'); } finally { f.cleanup(); }"

node --input-type=module -e "const { createControlPlaneFixture, writeFixtureServerScript } = await import('./test/helpers/control-plane-fixture.js'); const { spawnSync } = await import('node:child_process'); const f = createControlPlaneFixture(); let exitCode = 0; try { const script = writeFixtureServerScript(f.project); const checked = spawnSync(process.execPath, ['--check', script], { encoding: 'utf8', windowsHide: true }); if (checked.status !== 0) { console.error(checked.stderr || checked.stdout); exitCode = checked.status ?? 1; } } finally { f.cleanup(); } if (exitCode !== 0) process.exit(exitCode);"

failed_assumptions.assumption: Control-plane helper temp prefixes could use a new launchdeck-control-plane-* prefix.
failed_assumptions.evidence: Import smoke failed because cli-fixture removeTempProject only removes directories whose basename starts with launchdeck-cli-.
failed_assumptions.resolution: Changed helper prefixes to launchdeck-cli-control-plane-home- and launchdeck-cli-control-plane-project- so cleanup reuses the existing safety primitive.

failed_assumptions.assumption: writeFixtureServerScript did not need to return the generated file path.
failed_assumptions.evidence: Generated server script syntax smoke could not check an undefined path.
failed_assumptions.resolution: writeFixtureServerScript now returns the absolute path when the fixture exposes path(), and writeManagedFixtureProject includes absoluteScriptPath.

concerns: No current test imports this helper yet; evidence is limited to syntax, import/cleanup smoke, and generated server script syntax.
LSP diagnostics tooling is not exposed in this runtime, so node --check and import smoke were used for the modified JavaScript file.

consumer_evidence.example_usage: await withControlPlaneFixture(async (fixture) => { writeManagedFixtureProject(fixture.project, { name: 'demo', port: 4173 }); const added = fixture.runCliJson(['project', 'add']); const status = fixture.runGlobalCliJson(['status', '--all']); });
capability_notes: createLaunchdeckHome returns { homeDir, env, cleanup } with LAUNCHDECK_HOME isolated under a safe temp directory.
createControlPlaneFixture combines an isolated home and temp project root, wraps runCli/runCliJson with the isolated env, and exposes global CLI runners with cwd set to the home directory.
writeManagedFixtureProject writes .launchdeck.yml plus an actual script file such as scripts/dev-server.js instead of relying on inline long-running node -e commands.
fixtureServerScriptContent and writeFixtureServerScript provide reusable HTTP fixture server content for managed lifecycle and demo smoke tests.
cleanup reuses removeTempProject from cli-fixture.js, preserving the Windows EPERM retry behavior and existing safe temp-prefix guard.
ca_obligations: CA-CP-020 remains visible because generated managed fixtures use project-local script files suitable for future demo and cross-platform lifecycle smoke.
